H&M’s ongoing creative initiative, anchored in discovering and building the bridge between traditional and modern resources.
 
Venturing into new territory, the initiative explores the use of AI, introducing digital twins of professional models as both a creative extension and a behind-the scenes look at the artistic process. Each AI-generated double is developed in close collaboration with the model, who retains full control over their digital counterpart.
 
Throughout the fall, several creative expressions will be unveiled across different cities, exploring the possibilities of visual storytelling in close collaboration with the creative team. Still in its early, explorative phase, this serves as a primary fragment of an exciting, ongoing journey.

Lorena Saravia H&M marks H&M’s first-ever collaboration with a Mexican designer. The collection is an exciting milestone in H&M’s decades-long tradition of amplifying creativity, celebrating cultural identity and making meaningful design accessible worldwide.

“What I admire most about Lorena is how she transforms her heritage and Mexican craftsmanship into something fresh, original, and truly her own. There’s a strong connection to the earth that she balances beautifully with contemporary elegance.” — Ann-Sofie Johansson, Creative Advisor at H&M. 

The 29 piece collection launches 16 October. 
Worn by @jesssalgadoo

Lorena Saravia H&M is a dialogue with identity that honours the past while inhabiting the present. Details are drawn from personal histories and textures of everyday life in Mexico: intricate cut-outs were inspired by papel picado, woven leather echos petate mats and heirlooms are reimagined as jewellery and buttons.

The 28-piece collection launches 16 October.
Worn by @jesssalgadoo

“I think what you wear shows your personality. It’s a definition of what’s inside you.” — Lorena Saravia

The 28-piece collection launches 16 October. 
Worn by @jesssalgadoo

A shared step towards a regenerative future.
Presenting a collaboration where we take action towards regenerative cotton by joining forces with Materra and its mission to give nature a voice. This represents not just a commitment to better farming practices, but a shared vision of restoring balance between people, land, and the ecosystems that sustain us: an ongoing and collective step.
Discover more on this regenerative journey on mango.com. 
#MangoWoman

As India is one of three regions where cotton originated, Materra has learnt so much from these smallholder farmers. At Mango we value this combination of tradition and innovation in our sourcing, to create more products based on these regenerative practices.
Find out more in the Discover by section on our app.
#MangoWoman

Regenerative agriculture honors the connection between nature and our health. Materra works with India’s smallholder farmers, key stewards of the land, combining traditional knowledge and modern science to grow regenerative cotton. 
We complete the journey with pieces made from 50% MATERRA® cotton. 
Discover the pieces on mango.com.

Art In Progress | For Maria Yelletisch, a masterpiece is never complete—it is always evolving, much like the art of living itself.
 
Her work is a journey beyond time, where nature, light, and emotion weave together in a symphony of reflection. Each piece reveals the meditative power of recurrence—a rhythm echoing the pulse of the world around us. Through her eyes, the familiar transforms into a space of transcendence, where memory and sensation converge to create ‘safe places’—sanctuaries of connection that invite a deeper sense of harmony.
 
Immerse yourself in Maria Yelletisch’s essence at our flagship store, Paseo de Gracia 96, Barcelona.

FRAMED HERITAGE | Exploring Material and Meaning​
​
During Dubai Design Week 2025, we presented Framed Heritage, an immersive installation at Jossa, Alserkal Avenue.​
​
Through archival garments, accessories and images, we explored the dialogue between material and meaning — how leather, a defining element of our language, carries both memory and modernity.​
​
Visitors were invited to pause, observe and experience a space where heritage and presence coexist; a landscape of tactility and reflection that embodies our ongoing relationship with design.​
​

The Order Of Things | Shanghai
 
We open the doors to “The Order Of Things” — an immersive space crafted with interior designer Xie Ke, exploring how design, light and proportion create a quiet sense of belonging.
 
A reflection on the balance between craft, time and perception. Each piece reveals a precise tension between what is made and what is felt, transforming everyday elements into a subtle narrative of intention and refinement.
This sanctuary of balance at No.181 invites a slow gaze into details. A contemporary reflection on how matter, light and proportion define the brand’s enduring essence, shaping a quiet place that feels like home.
 
Opened on November 22 in Shanghai.
 
@wallpapermag
@xieke.505 
 
#MassimoDutti

In The Order Of Things, the collaboration with Xie Ke takes center stage. His minimal, poetic vision defines the atmosphere: a study in balance, materiality and quiet tension.​
​
​Within the installation, each piece acts as a point of focus. Objects that hold space rather than fill it, guiding the eye through shadow, volume and proportion.​
​
His work introduces an essential calm to the environment, grounding Massimo Dutti’s contemporary aesthetic in a space where design becomes experience.​
​

A Forever Story | 90s Edit​
​
Some stories last forever.​
Together they capture the spirit of the 90s — timeless, refined and minimal.​
An iconic couple redefining everlonging style with quiet allure. Side by side, they embody a cool confidence rooted in subtle strength.​
Her wardrobe speaks through the power of contrast: black dresses, white tops and barely-there sandals.​
His style is defined by quiet strength: structured blazers, linen shirts, timeless moccasins. ​
A modern expression of 90s poise.​
​
They don’t follow trends.​
They move with natural ease, shaping a language of classic pieces and effortless attitude.​
A forever kind of story.​
​
Starring: @liz.kennedy_ & @qt_coco

FANGLU LIN X ZARA
Fanglu Lin brings her visionary artistry into the realm of fashion through an exclusive collaboration with Zara — marking her first-ever venture into wearable design. The result is a capsule collection for women, men, children and home that fuses ancestral artistry with contemporary sensibility, transforming the language of textile art into a new form of expression.
 
Models: Chu Wong & Yingge Yang
Photographer: Chris Rhodes 
Location: Yangshuo, Guilin (China)
 
#FangluLinxZara #zara

FANGLU LIN X ZARA
The collection is rich with the traditional textile vocabulary that first captured her imagination: embroidery, knotting, ruching, shirring, and of course, dyeing. Deconstructed tailoring is defined by intricate cord details—anchoring necklines, shaping silhouettes, and lending sculptural dimension to fluid garments. Elsewhere, her signature techniques create surfaces like living textiles: textured, layered, and protective, yet full of movement and grace. Knotted elements and gathering define dresses that gently contour the body, simultaneously revealing and concealing with poetic restraint.
 
Models: Chu Wong & Yingge Yang
Photographer: Chris Rhodes 
Location: Yangshuo, Guilin (China)
 
#FangluLinxZara #zara

FANGLU LIN X ZARA
Fanglu’s first-ever fashion collections are completed in a children’s line that mirrors her ethos—playful, innovative, and soulfully designed.

Raw yet refined, elemental yet exquisitely detailed, this capsule collection distills the essence of Fanglu Lin’s contemporary femininity. Most profoundly, it brings her art into daily life—accessible, wearable, and alive in motion.

Photographer: Chris Rhodes 
Location: Yangshuo, Guilin (China)

#FangluLinxZara #zara

50 SONGS OF THE SEA
A celebration of 50 years of Zara

The observatory at the top of Monte de San Pedro in A Coruña is transformed into a kinetic installation that encompasses viewers within a luminous visual poem to mark ZARA’s 50th year, created by British artist and designer Es Devlin @esdevlin 

#ZARA50 #50SongsOfTheSea

50 SONGS OF THE SEA
A celebration of 50 years of Zara
 
As part of its 50th anniversary celebrations, Zara inaugurates a large kinetic installation in A Coruña created by artist Es Devlin @esdevlin and narration by Elena Anaya @elenaanayaoficial
 
The work responds to the energy of this historic Galician site – a rugged promontory that juts into the waves and weather systems of the atlantic ocean – marked by the broad reaching beams of the legendary unesco protected tower of hercules.
 
Visitors will encounter 360 degree views from the observatory - poised between vistas that reach south towards the depths of Spain and Africa, east across Europe, and west across the atlantic towards the Americas.
 
Within the observatory’s 25 metre diameter domed interior, 250 seats are set within 3 concentric revolving turntables. Visitors will rotate around one another, as they are inmersed in a visual installation that celebrates this milestone in Zara’s history, transmitted through poetry, dance, music and light.
 
#ZARA50 #50SongsOfTheSea

50 SONGS OF THE SEA
A celebration of 50 years of Zara
Zara, continues its anniversary celebrations with the restoration and reopening of A Coruña’s Cúpula Atlántica Observatory overlooking the city and its impressive coastline from Monte de
San Pedro.
Inside this space, visitors will find a spectacular kinetic installation entitled ‘50 Songs of the Sea’, created by British artist and designer Es Devlin @esdevlin for the interior of the Observatory's 28-metre diameter dome.
#ZARA50 #50SongsOfTheSea

ZARA SRPLS LINEN COLLECTION
A new capsule dedicated to bringing the SRPLS philosophy of “Beautility” – that sweet spot between ease and elevation – to high summer.

The primary material in nearly all of the garments is linen, specially blended and treated to retain its natural breathability and moisture-wicking properties while also retaining its shape and surface smoothness.

ZARA SRPLS LINEN COLLECTION
The “Linen” menswear capsule includes several distinct cuts of roomily draped utility pants. T-shirts and sweater vests are perforated to enable the body to breathe. A garment-dyed linen-blend tailored jacket and matching deconstructed cargo pants make an ideal off-duty uniform for nonchalant midsummer assignments. Striped, flap-pocketed linen popover tunics and a wide-sleeved camp-collar shirt with loose-legged trousers in head-to-toe khaki linen are further fresh and versatile options. Accessories include sunglasses, deflip-flops and beaded jewellery.

ZARA SRPLS LINEN COLLECTION
Kidswear for girls and boys is designed to enable play and carefree confidence. Unfussily robust shorts, tops, shirts, pinafore dresses, bucket hats, and sleeveless all-in-ones all echo the atmosphere of earthy optimism and effortless elevation of the adult collections.

Pulled together, casually refined, but never over-considered, SRPLS “Linen” brings a fresh approach to dressing for the warmest days of summer.

50 SONGS OF THE SEA
A celebration of 50 years of Zara
 
Created by Es Devlin @esdevlin, 50 Songs of the Sea reimagined the Cúpula Atlántica in A Coruña as a kinetic installation celebrating Zara’s 50th anniversary. More than 100,000 visitors took part in this immersive experience, a tribute to the ocean, the city, and five decades of creativity.
 
Throughout the months of celebration, the Cúpula also hosted an extraordinary concert series featuring artists such as Myles Smith, Air, The Blaze, Luz Casal, and Patti Smith, bringing together music, movement, and light in a celebration that marked half a century of Zara.
 
To everyone who created, collaborated, and experienced 50 Songs of the Sea, thank you.
 
#ZARA50 #50SongsOfTheSea

STUDIO COLLECTION FW25
Keepers of the Horizon
 
The kidswear story centres on British country dress. Soft tweed jackets with patch pockets sit over kilted skirts or tartan shorts; corduroy coats are cut with rounded collars; duffles are trimmed with toggles and sleeve turn-backs. Shirts carry oxford stripes or micro florals. Knitwear includes oatmeal fair isles, mossy cables, and patterned yokes, sometimes layered over collared shirting and worn with chinos or cords. Bucket hats, ribbed beanies and wellington boots complete the portfolio.

INTO THE PROCESS
THE PERFECT WARDROBE
 
By Alexandre Silberstein @alexandresilberstein
Starring ZARA Shoes and bags
 
In-house Team, Spain.
 
Inspiration, leather selection, touch, design, model, sketches, technical sheet, last, modelling, centimetre, inch, precision, prototype, correction, measurements, gaze, assembly test, intuition, cutting, fitting, observation.
 
Concepts that accompany us throughout our journey of technical creation and design. “Into the Process” tells the stories behind the development of prototypes from a selection of Zara Woman shoes and bags, created under the attentive eye of a meticulous process guided by experience, precision, and creativity.
 
Six pieces conceived to be part of a scheme of timeless, high-quality essentials: three pairs of shoes and three bags, indispensable for everyday life, which together form the collection “The Perfect Wardrobe.”

ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 15 / DROP I 

AW25’s women’s line is built for maximum versatility and minimal fuss: it contains field and trench lineages cut with ease through the arms and chest; compact knits and crisp poplins that sharpen the silhouette; skirts and trousers calibrated to clear a boot and move. The fashion language is direct and day-driven, yet multiple looks translate smoothly into evening; coats with a long, clean fall over a narrow skirt; a bomber-based piece whose sheen and structure amplify impact. Hardware is always deployed for a reason: an integrated belt sets the waist in seconds; a hem adjuster decides volume. The result is elevation without complication.

Photographer: Craig McDean @craigmcdeanstudio

ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 15 / DROP I 
 
This season draws on the mid-century foundations of Ivy League archetypes and mid-50s proportion then retools them for present-tense wear. Field and flight architectures are pared back; textured knits add depth to fundamental uniforms of coat, knit, trouser, boot. Central to the season is SRPLS’s signature detail-rich full-leg trouser made straightforward. The rise is balanced, the thigh is generous for movement, and the hem is set to meet a boot or cinch for shape. It’s a proportion-setter: pull it on and the silhouette resolves instantly. Pockets, drawcords, and fastenings are specified to earn their place, turning familiar pieces into daily equipment rather than ornament. This is a collection populated by a competing cast of daily must-wears.

ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 15 / DROP I 
 
The season’s kidswear offer reflects the same core intent: these pieces made to move, with trims chosen for comfort and robustness, in fabrics that tolerate real life. Field shells are light but protective. Liners are warm without bulk. From school to the weekend, this is a wardrobe for girls and boys that enables fuss-free play and self-expression: fashionable fit-for-purposefulness.

AARON LEVINE x ZARA

Aaron P. Levine @aaronplevine has been captivated by clothing since the age of four. As a child, he would arrange pieces on the floor, experimenting with colors, textures, and fabrics—never with the intention of wearing them, but simply to observe the dialogue they created together. 

Over the years, he has honed an instinctive mastery for blending items across diverse product categories, assembling looks that feel at once rooted in tradition and refreshingly unexpected. Tailored garments, technical outerwear, denim, knitwear, and trekking boots converge effortlessly under his eye, forming functional, considered outfits suited to virtually any setting.

For him, this practice is more than a craft; it is a lifelong devotion—his profession, and his passion.

#zara #zaraman #aaronlevine

SOSHIOTSUKI x ZARA

Introducing 'A Sense of Togetherness', a collaboration that spans womenswear, menswear, and kidswear. Rooted in a nostalgia for Japan’s 1980s and 1990s cultural landscape, this collection explores universal aspects of human connection: family ties, generational connection, and relationships that endure. The collection blends a delicate, vintage sensibility with a precise and modern outlook.

#SoshiotsukiXZara @soshiotsuki

SOSHIOTSUKI x ZARA

Each line reflects a different aspect of Otsuki’s vision. WOMAN reinterprets tailoring through fluid shapes and soft proportions, with a palette that is both nostalgic and contemporary. MAN exhibits Otsuki’s virtuoso balance of structure and ease, featuring refined cuts, thoughtful draping, and formal fabrics used in unexpected ways. KIDS brings intimacy to the forefront, echoing the handmade pieces Otsuki recalls from his own formative years. 

#SoshiotsukiXZara @soshiotsuki

SOSHIOTSUKI x ZARA

Although casual pieces appear across the collection, the designer deliberately chose fabrics with formal roots. The result is a collection that’s both polished and gently emotional. Into the Process VII

Inspiration, fabric selection, touch, design, model, sketches, garment technical sheet, pattern making, modeling, centimeter, inch, scaling, prototype, rectification, measurements, looking, ironing, intuition, cutting, fitting, observation. Concepts that accompany us on our journey of pattern creation and design.
 
A selection of Zara Woman garments that are created under the watchful eye of a careful process.
 
The process behind each of these pieces marks a constant, revealing and significant path in the development of prototypes.

ZARA AND JOHN GALLIANO ANNOUNCE A TWO-YEAR CREATIVE PARTNERSHIP.
 
Zara announces a two-year creative partnership with John Galliano, who will re-author the brand’s archives through a series of seasonal collections.
 
Mr. Galliano will be working directly with garments from Zara’s past seasons, deconstructing and reconfiguring them into new seasonal expressions and creations.
 
Guided by a couture process and authorship, the collections will be released seasonally over the course of the partnership beginning in September 2026.
 
Further details will be announced in due course.
 
Photographer / Art Director: Szilveszter Makó @szilvesztermako

STUDIO COLLECTION SS26
Delicately Distressed

For Spring/Summer 2026, Zara Studio womenswear reinvents contemporary romantic dressing through a fresh approach to colours, layers and contrasts. Washed floral slip dresses edged with antique-style lace are worn beneath softly cut tweed coats and striped lounge tailoring. A pale mint lace-insert skirt paired with a cropped white tee, a pink-checked fringed suit styled over a camisole, and an ivory tiered dress finished with lace bands and worn with silver T-bar kitten heels define the collection’s powdered, gentle toned mood.
